title: rubus × hypoleucanthemus
intro: Rubus × hypoleucanthemus, also known as the raspberry guelder rose or thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that combines the characteristics of raspberries (Rubus spp.) and guelder roses (Viburnum opulus). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × hypoleucanthemus are said to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Rubus × hypoleucanthemus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for consumption, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
